content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To    : File 86107 and 86109                    Date  : Feb. 3, 1978


From  : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
        Bob Stern

Subject: Reporting of a Gift to a Lobbyist


        The lobbyist for the State Building and Construction
Trades Council has been offered a trip by Southern
California Gas Company to Japan to inspect a Japanese
LNG operation. This trip will be helpful to the lobbyist
in his efforts to lobby the legislature for an LNG
terminal in California.

        Question:

        1.    Does the lobbyist have to report the receipt of
the trip? If so, on which part of the form?

        The opinion request meeting concluded that both the
lobbyist and Southern California Gas Company would have
to list the value of the trip on their respective forms.
The lobbyist should include the trip from Southern
California Gas Company on "Payments Received in Connec-
tion with Lobbying Activity," column (c) - "Advances or
Other Receipts."

        2.    Does Southern California Gas Company have to
report its payment for the trip? If so, on what part of
the form?

        The lobbyist employer should include the trip and
the name of the lobbyist on its form in Part 2(a) "Pay-
ments aggregating $25 or more this period for services
provided by other than the filer or the filer's employees."

sections.question:
1. 
Does the lobbyist have to report the receipt of 
the trip? If so, on which part of the form? 
The opinion request meeting concluded that both the

sections.conclusion: The opinion request meeting concluded that both the lobbyist and Southern California Gas Company would have to list the value of the trip on their respective forms. The lobbyist should include the trip from Southern California Gas Company on "Payments Received in Connection with Lobbying Activity," column (c) - "Advances or Other Receipts." The lobbyist employer should include the trip and the name of the lobbyist on its form in Part 2(a) "Payments aggregating $25 or more this period for services provided by other than the filer or the filer's employees."
sections.question_synthetic: A lobbyist has been offered a trip to Japan by Southern California Gas Company to inspect an LNG operation relevant to his lobbying work. The question asks whether the lobbyist and the company must report this gift, and if so, on which forms and sections.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Both the lobbyist and Southern California Gas Company must report the trip's value on their respective forms. The lobbyist reports it under "Payments Received in Connection with Lobbying Activity" in the "Advances or Other Receipts" column, while the company reports it in Part 2(a) under payments for services by non-employees.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document is a formal FPPC advice letter with clear Question and Conclusion sections. File numbers 86107 and 86109 referenced. Response authored by Bob Stern from FPPC.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1. 
Does the lobbyist have to report the receipt of 
the trip? If so, on which part of the form? 
The opinion request meeting concluded that both the 
lobbyist and Southern California Gas Company would have 
to list the value of the trip on their respective forms. 
The lobbyist should include the trip from Southern 
California Gas Company on "Payments Received in Connec-
tion with Lobbying Activity," column (c) -
"Advances or 
Other Receipts." 
2. 
Does Southern California Gas Company have to 
report its payment for the trip? If so, on what part of 
the form? 
The lobbyist employer should include the trip and 
the name of the lobbyist on its form in Part 2(a) "Pay-
ments aggregating $25 or more this period for services 
provided by other than the filer or the filer's employees."

CONCLUSION: The opinion request meeting concluded that both the lobbyist and Southern California Gas Company would have to list the value of the trip on their respective forms. The lobbyist should include the trip from Southern California Gas Company on "Payments Received in Connection with Lobbying Activity," column (c) - "Advances or Other Receipts." The lobbyist employer should include the trip and the name of the lobbyist on its form in Part 2(a) "Payments aggregating $25 or more this period for services provided by other than the filer or the filer's employees."

This trip will be helpful to the lobbyist in his efforts to lobby the legislature for an LNG terminal in California. Question: 1. Does the lobbyist have to report the receipt of the trip? If so, on which part of the form? The opinion request meeting concluded that both the lobbyist and Southern California Gas Company would have to list the value of the trip on their respective forms. embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter addressing gift reporting requirements for a lobbyist offered a trip to Japan by Southern California Gas Company. Both the lobbyist and the company are required to report the trip's value on their respective lobbying forms.
